title: Howardia pilosa

intro: Howardia pilosa, also known as toothache plant, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health conditions, including toothaches, digestive issues, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits, such as analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Howardia pilosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Howardia pilosa is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in topical applications to alleviate pain and inflammation.
